activities_projects.title: Laws of Reflection Verification
activities_projects.objective: Verify the laws of reflection using a plane mirror
materials: Laser pointer or torch with narrow beam
steps: Place the plane mirror vertically on white paper
Draw the normal and incident ray at different angles
Measure angle of incidence and reflection
Tabulate and confirm i = r
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands and verifies the laws of reflection experimentally
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of measurements and conclusion (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Image Formation in Plane Mirror
activities_projects.objective: Observe characteristics of images formed by plane mirrors
steps: Place object in front of mirror
Locate image position using ray method or no-parallax method
Measure object distance, image distance, and size
Note lateral inversion and virtual nature
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes properties like virtual, erect, same size, and laterally inverted images

activities_projects.title: Concave Mirror Ray Diagram Poster
activities_projects.objective: Illustrate image formation by concave mirrors for different object positions
steps: Draw ray diagrams for object at infinity, beyond C, at C, between C and F, at F, between F and P
Label principal axis, focus, center of curvature, image position, nature
Write real-life uses (e.g., solar cooker, shaving mirror)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands image characteristics for concave mirrors

activities_projects.title: Convex Mirror Rear-View Demonstration
activities_projects.objective: Demonstrate field of view advantage of convex mirrors
materials: Convex mirror (small makeup or toy mirror)
steps: Observe field of view in plane vs convex mirror
Place objects at different angles and note visibility
Record why convex mirrors are used in vehicles
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Appreciates wide field of view and virtual, diminished images in convex mirrors

activities_projects.title: Refraction through Glass Slab
activities_projects.objective: Observe lateral shift in refraction through glass slab
steps: Place slab on paper and trace outline
Draw incident ray at angle, mark emergent ray
Measure angle of incidence, refraction, and lateral shift
Repeat for different angles
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands refraction, bending of light, and apparent depth/shift
activities_projects.evaluation: Ray tracing accuracy and shift calculation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Apparent Depth Experiment
activities_projects.objective: Verify apparent depth in water and calculate refractive index
steps: Measure real depth of coin in empty beaker
Fill with water and measure apparent depth by looking from top
Calculate refractive index = real depth / apparent depth
Repeat with different depths
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Learns concept of apparent depth and refractive index

activities_projects.title: Convex Lens Image Formation Activity
activities_projects.objective: Study real image formation by convex lens
steps: Set up lens and move object/screen to get sharp image
Record object distance (u), image distance (v), nature for different positions
Draw ray diagrams for confirmation
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands image formation rules for convex lens
activities_projects.evaluation: Table of observations and ray diagrams (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Refractive Index of Different Media
activities_projects.objective: Compare refractive index of air, water, and glass
steps: Pass laser beam through each medium at same angle
Measure angle of refraction
Calculate refractive index (sin i / sin r)
Compare values
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Learns that refractive index depends on medium

activities_projects.title: Mirror and Lens Uses Collage
activities_projects.objective: Identify real-life applications of mirrors and lenses
steps: Collect/cut pictures of concave mirror (torch, headlight), convex mirror (vehicle), convex lens (magnifier, spectacles), concave lens (myopia correction)
Paste and label with uses and chapter concepts
Write short notes on each
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects chapter concepts to daily life applications

activities_projects.title: Simple Periscope Model
activities_projects.objective: Build a model to demonstrate reflection in periscope
steps: Cut holes in box for viewing and object
Fix mirrors at 45° angle inside box
Test by viewing object around corner
Explain using laws of reflection
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies laws of reflection to real optical device
